_metadata.purpose: Maps regional synonyms and disambiguation parentheticals in region_grapes.json's signature field to their canonical-101 names. Used by the Grape Resonances aggregator at recurrence-count time so that the same grape carrying the same temperament under different regional names counts as one finding.
_metadata.scope: Signature-tier entries only (v1 uses Tier 1 only). Lesser-known and rare tiers are not consulted by the v1 aggregator and so need not be aliased here.
_metadata.rule: An alias is included only when source and target are the same grape variety. Regional spelling variants of the same grape (Spätburgunder, Shiraz, Pinot Grigio, Tinto Fino, Cannonau, Garnatxa, Macabeu→[no canonical], etc.) are aliased. Distinct but related grapes (Pinot Meunier vs Pinot Noir; Carricante vs Catarratto) are NOT aliased — they are different varieties even where genetically related.
_metadata.silent_skip: Signature grapes that are neither in the canonical 101 nor in this alias table are silently dropped from the response pool. Per the v1 scope decision (PROJECT.md 2026-05-10), the canonical 101 is the user-facing set; some regions (notably Etna, Liguria, Goriška Brda, Jura, Champagne) thereby contribute no v1 grape. The trace fold still shows the matched region; it just does not surface a grape from it.
